claim: A current Etsy minimalist vase STL listing uses explicit digital-download disclosure, modern and Scandinavian decor language, print settings, use cases, and a visible sale price of US$2.40 from US$4.00; the page showed two item reviews and 19 favorites when accessed.
notes: Direct observation of one listing, not market-wide demand or a stable price benchmark.

claim: A current Etsy modern planter STL listing presents support-free printing, digital delivery, personal-use licensing, and an external commercial-license path; its visible sale price was US$3.24 from US$5.90 when crawled.
notes: Single-listing evidence supports packaging and licensing patterns, not profitability.

claim: Etsy's arch-tray market page mixed physical products, molds, SVGs, and 3D-print files and displayed multiple jewelry, entryway, shelf-decor, and gift use cases.
notes: Useful for language and category adjacency; mixed formats prevent a clean STL demand estimate.

claim: Etsy's arch-jewelry-tray page showed physical trays, 3D-printed trays, molds, and digital STL listings with repeated jewelry, perfume, vanity, nightstand, and entryway positioning.
notes: Supports use-case breadth and competition presence; results are regional and mixed-product.

claim: A Cults3D minimalist-vase page showed two STL files, dimensions, 946 views, 29 downloads, four likes, and no makes at access time.
notes: Platform metrics are direct for one older free design; they do not establish paid resin-STL demand.

claim: A Cults3D modern rectangle planter offered separate drainage and non-drainage STL files and showed 8.7k views, 526 downloads, 47 likes, and two comments at access time; the description said supports were required.
notes: Demonstrates variant utility and visible engagement for a free FDM-oriented model, not resin economics.

claim: A 2025 Cults3D minimalist vase-decor listing bundled two vase files with a base tray, drainage, no-support positioning, use cases, dimensions, and a link to a broader vase-and-planter collection.
notes: Direct packaging evidence; stated print claims belong to the seller and are not Atlas validation.

claim: A Cults3D abstract cutout-vase page offered two STL variants and showed 4.7k views, 429 downloads, 100 likes, two collections, and no makes at access time.
notes: Shows visible engagement and multi-variant packaging for one open-priced design; zero makes limits print-success evidence.

claim: No direct current marketplace evidence was collected for Quiet Architectural resin card stands, display risers, relief tiles, or desk sculptures.
notes: These concepts remain hypotheses requiring targeted research and prototype validation.
